finding: ATM-GOV-0194 delivered diff planning, persistent TypeScript build-info plumbing, decision classes, and focused tests, but no real dogfood run has yet produced `incrementalBuild`. Common framework changes that include any script still fall back to a roughly 40-second full rebuild, even when the script is only a validator and package changes are localized.
expectedBehavior: Runner release sync should support cache-miss incremental builds: affected package detection, persistent TypeScript build info, package-level dist updates, hash-based root-drop copy-if-changed, onefile payload reuse, and receipts distinguishing cacheHitSkip, incrementalBuild, and fullRebuild.
evidenceOrRepro: `tests/cli/runner-sync-incremental-build.test.ts` passes, but scanning `.atm/runtime/telemetry/runner-sync-build/**` finds zero `incrementalBuild` records. ATM-GOV-0194 recorded fullRebuild=43.127s because the card changed build scripts. ATM-GOV-0195 recorded fullRebuild=41.377s with reason `build-script-change` for three telemetry package files plus `scripts/validate-gate-telemetry-coverage.ts`; worktree setup was 8.884s, TypeScript 15.368s, root-drop 7.477s, and artifact sync 7.943s.
followUp: Keep the 0194 implementation but add a real package-only dogfood benchmark and narrow unsafe script classification by dependency impact. Then optimize the dominant phases independently: reusable sealed worktree, true package/dependent compilation, affected root-drop subtree sync, and artifact copy-if-changed. Do not close the performance claim until at least one changed-source run is measurably faster than the full baseline.
